TransL2E 1.0.0
  • Reference
  • Articles
    • TransL2E_Demo

    TransL2E_Demo

    TransL2E_Demo.Rmd

    Package Install 

    # install TransL2E package from github
if (!requireNamespace("TransL2E", quietly = TRUE)) {
    remotes::install_github("Xiaoqian-Liu/TransL2E")
}

library(TransL2E)

# load library for Trans_GLM
library(glmnet)
library(glmtrans)

# some logistic package
library(MASS)
library(stats)
library(tibble)

    Sparse Regression

    Here we present a small example to showcase the usage of our TransL2E package under the sparse regression. The example consists of a sparse linear regression with \(p = 100\) parameters and a target task (\(n_t = 200\)), supplemented by \(K = 5\) source datasets, each with \(n_s = 400\) observations. The target coefficient vector \(\beta_t\) is characterized by 10% sparsity, while the relationship between sources is governed by a model shift (\(\sigma_{ms} = 0.2\)) and a covariate shift (\(\rho = 0.5\)). To further challenge the estimation accuracy, the data includes a 10% outlier proportion.

    # a function to simulate the covariance matrix
source_cov = function(p, rho){
  
  source_covariance = matrix(0, nrow = p, ncol = p)
  for (i in 1:p) {
    for (j in 1:p) {
      source_covariance[i,j] = rho^abs(i-j)
    }
  }
  return(source_covariance)
}

# a function to create given number of source datasets
source_cre <- function(n_s, p, p_s, beta_t, snr, sigma_s, Sigma_S, K, out_prop, out_sig) {
  
  # Generate a list of K source datasets
  source_list <- lapply(seq_len(K), function(i) {
    # Generate design matrix for the current source
    X <- mvrnorm(n = n_s, mu = rep(0, p), Sigma = Sigma_S)
    
    # Create a shift vector for beta
    delta_s <- rep(0, p)
    shift_index = sample(1:p, p_s, replace = F)
    delta_s[shift_index] <- rnorm(p_s, mean = 0, sd = sigma_s)
    #delta_s[1:p_s] <- rnorm(p_s, mean = 0, sd = sigma_s)
    
    # Adjust beta_t with the current shift to obtain beta_s
    beta_s <- beta_t + delta_s
    
    # Generate response vector Y with additive noise
    Y <- X %*% beta_s + rnorm(n_s, mean = 0, sd = snr)
    
    # Introduce outliers to a proportion of the responses
    n_out <- floor(n_s * out_prop)
    Y[1:n_out] <- Y[1:n_out] + out_sig*max(abs(Y))
    
    # Introduce outliers to a proportion of the covariates
    #X[1:n_out, ] = X[1:n_out, ] + out_sig
    
    # Return the source dataset in the desired list format
    list(x = X, y = Y)
  })
  
  return(source_list)
}


# define parameters
sigma_snr = 1

p = 100 # parameters
n_t = 200 # target sample size
n_s = 400 # source sample size

K = 5 # number of source

rho = 0.5 # level of covariate shift
Sigma = source_cov(p, rho)

beta_t = matrix(0, nrow = p)
beta_t[1:(0.1*p), 1] = 1 # 10% sparsity

sigma_ms = 0.2 # model shift level
p_s = 0.3*p

# outlier proportion and signal level
out_prop = 0.1
out_sig = 2

# lambda seuence
lambda_seq = exp(seq(log(10), log(10^-4), length.out = 20))

set.seed(1)
# simulate the target sample
X_t = matrix(rnorm(n_t * p), nrow = n_t, ncol = p) # target covariates
y_t = X_t %*% beta_t + rnorm(n_t, mean = 0, sd = sigma_snr) # target response
    
# add outler to the samples
y_t[1:(n_t * out_prop)] = y_t[1:(n_t * out_prop)] + out_sig*max(y_t)

# simulate the source dataset
cur_data = source_cre(n_s = n_s, p = p, p_s = p_s, beta_t = beta_t,
                      snr = sigma_snr, sigma_s = sigma_ms, Sigma_S = Sigma,
                      K = K, out_prop = out_prop, out_sig = out_sig)

    Base L2E with Target Data Only

    Here we fit a L2E regression with the target dataset only, which serves as a base case. To fit a L2E sparse regression model, we use CV_L2E_sparse_ncv function from our TransL2E package. And we use Lasso penalty here.

    set.seed(1)
# Run Base L2E on Target Data Only
Time1_start = proc.time()
    
# Fit the l2e lasso model
sol_mm_cv <- CV_L2E_sparse_ncv(y_t, X_t, penalty = 'lasso', nfolds = 5, trace = FALSE)

      
sol_mm_opt = L2E_sparse_ncv(y_t, X_t, lambdaSeq = sol_mm_cv$lambda.1se, penalty = 'lasso', Show.Time = FALSE)
      
Time_plain_l2e = (proc.time() - Time1_start)[[3]]

# square error for parameter estimation
plain_l2e = norm(beta_t - sol_mm_opt$Beta, '2')/norm(beta_t, '2')

print(paste0('Base L2E Estimation Error: ', round(plain_l2e, 3)))
    #> [1] "Base L2E Estimation Error: 0.152"

    Base glmnet Lasso with Target Data Only

    Similarly, we also compare with a naive Lasso fit with target data only, which is done via glmnet.

    set.seed(1)
# Run glmnet with lasso on target
Time2_start = proc.time()
    
# do a baseline lasso regression with glmnet
cv_lasso <- cv.glmnet(X_t, y_t, alpha = 1, lambdaSeq = lambda_seq, intercept = FALSE, nfolds = 5)
lasso_best <- glmnet(X_t, y_t, alpha = 1, lambda = cv_lasso$lambda.1se, intercept = FALSE)
    
Time_plain_lasso = (proc.time() - Time2_start)[[3]]
    
    
beta_plain_lasso = as.vector(coef(lasso_best))[-1]
    
# square error for parameter estimation
plain_lasso = norm(beta_t - beta_plain_lasso, '2')/norm(beta_t, '2')

print(paste0('Base Lasso Estimation Error: ', round(plain_lasso, 3)))
    #> [1] "Base Lasso Estimation Error: 1"

    Trans_L2E

    To fit a transfer learning sparse regression under L2E loss, we use the TL_L2E_sparse function from our package. Parameter debias controls whether we want to perform debiasing step or not. lambda_detect is the hyperparameter list for source sample detection. lambda_colearn is the hyperparameter list for co-learning, and lambda_debias is the hyperparameter list for debiasing (if set debias = 1). (y_t, X_t) records the target dataset information, and the source information should be input via source_list.

    # Run TransL2E
Time3_start <- proc.time()

Trans_L2E_estimation <- TL_L2E_sparse(y_t, X_t, source_list = cur_data, core = 1, debias = TRUE, seed = 1,
                                lambda_detect = "lambda.1se", lambda_colearn="lambda.1se",
                                lambda_debias="lambda.min")
    #> Step 1: Source Detection
    #> Step 2: Co-learning
    #> Step 3: Debiasing
    Time_with_debias <- (proc.time() - Time3_start)["elapsed"]
Trans_L2E_error <- norm(beta_t - Trans_L2E_estimation$Coefficients, "2")/norm(beta_t, '2')


# Record source selection proportion
source_prop <- sum(sapply(Trans_L2E_estimation$Selected_Source, function(src) nrow(src$y))) / (n_s * K)

print(paste0('TransL2E Estimation Error: ', round(Trans_L2E_error, 3)))
    #> [1] "TransL2E Estimation Error: 0.11"
    print(paste0('TransL2E Source Data Selection Proportion: ', round(source_prop, 3)))  
    #> [1] "TransL2E Source Data Selection Proportion: 0.449"

    Trans_GLM

    Finally we compare the transfer learning results with the trans-GLM method, which is done via the glmtrans function.

    set.seed(1)
# Run transfer glm with gaussian distribution
Time4_start = proc.time()

# run Transfer glm function
fit.tlglm <- glmtrans(list(x = X_t, y = y_t), cur_data, intercept = FALSE, 
                      detection.info = TRUE, nfolds = 5)
    #> Loss difference between source data and the threshold: (negative to be transferable)
#> Source 1: -63.886473
#> Source 2: -60.117263
#> Source 3: -64.010204
#> Source 4: -61.223772
#> Source 5: -60.117263
#> 
#> Source data set(s) 1, 2, 3, 4, 5 are transferable!
    Time_tl_glm = (proc.time() - Time4_start)[[3]]
    
# beta estimate for tl_glm
beta_tlglm = as.vector(fit.tlglm$beta)[-1]
    
# square error for parameter estimation
tl_glm = norm(beta_t - beta_tlglm, '2')/norm(beta_t, '2')
    

source_num <- length(fit.tlglm$transfer.source.id)
    

print(paste0('Trans_GLM Estimation Error: ', round(tl_glm, 3)))
    #> [1] "Trans_GLM Estimation Error: 0.825"

    Results Summary

    Here is a table summarize the relative estimation error for each method.

    # combine result together

estimation_error = c(plain_l2e, plain_lasso, Trans_L2E_error, tl_glm)
Method_name = c('Base L2E', 'Glmnet Lasso', 'TransL2E', 'TransGLM')

results_table = tibble(Method = Method_name, Estimation_Error = estimation_error)
print(results_table)
    #> # A tibble: 4 × 2
#>   Method       Estimation_Error
#>   <chr>                   <dbl>
#> 1 Base L2E                0.152
#> 2 Glmnet Lasso            1    
#> 3 TransL2E                0.110
#> 4 TransGLM                0.825

    Contents
